headline: Prisoners' voting rights remain unaddressed since 2019 review
context: Recommendations to restore voting rights to prisoners from a 2019 Election Commission review have not been implemented as of 2025.

cards.big: 74,000 prisoners denied voting rights with no reform since 2019 review
cards.sub: EC recommended postal voting for prisoners but implementation stalled

cards.big: Election Offences Act 1954 does not explicitly disenfranchise prisoners
cards.sub: Practical barriers rather than legal prohibition prevent prisoner voting

cards.big: ICCPR Article 25 guarantees right to vote without unreasonable restrictions
cards.sub: European Court of Human Rights ruled blanket prisoner voting bans unlawful

cards.big: 74,000 imprisoned Malaysians represent 0.5% of total voter population
Imprisonment restricts liberty, it should not automatically erase citizenship
Bersih 2.0 included prisoner voting in 10-point electoral reform proposal for 2024
